fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Cloudflare API Key. Provide relevant read-only permissions

fields.label: Token
fields.description: Token for accessing the datasource API

instructions.msg: Cloudflare configuration is stored under a service-keyed jsonData shape: jsonData.services.cloudflare.* holds per-service settings, while secrets use flat dotted secureJsonData keys of the form "cloudflare.<secret>". There is a single service with id 'cloudflare'.

Authentication uses a Cloudflare API Key/token (bearer) with relevant read-only permissions. jsonData.services.cloudflare.auth.id is 'bearer_token' (the backend defaults to it) and the token is the write-only secret at secureJsonData key "cloudflare.token".

Minimal working payload: {"jsonData":{"services":{"cloudflare":{"auth":{"id":"bearer_token"}}}},"secureJsonData":{"cloudflare.token":"<cloudflare-api-token>"}}. Create the token at https://dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens.
Connection: the server URL is fixed at https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4 (no server selector and no connection variables).

The secret at secureJsonData key "cloudflare.token" is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether it is already configured, read secureJsonFields["cloudflare.token"] == true rather than secureJsonData.
